purpose: Current production bundle debt ceiling. The v3.51.26 payroll export integrity boundary measures 4,600,314 raw / 1,320,496 gzip JavaScript bytes on the reviewed Windows source tree. Against the v3.51.25 reviewed artifact (4,587,149 / 1,316,233), the delta is +13,165 raw / +4,263 gzip (under 0.33%) for the pure export orchestrator, fail-closed entitlement and tenant-scope handling, bidirectional mutation/export single-flight gate, localized accessible recovery states, async web/native delivery port, complete RPC pagination, canonical projection, UUID/timestamp/value/arithmetic validation and targeted error recovery. The largest chunk measures 1,789,794 raw / 569,555 gzip (+4,644 / +1,606). No database or Edge wire contract, runtime dependency, CSS size or route was added. The reviewed ceilings retain only 128 raw / 279 gzip bytes of cross-platform and release-SHA tolerance. Further increases require an explicit measured baseline review.
